metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1105
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Rab35 functions together with its GAP, Skywalker, to regulate the sorting of synaptic vesicle membranes through synaptic endosomes via an ESCRT-dependent mechanism.

In sky mutants, FM1-43 was observed to accumulate in RAB5- and synaptotagmin 1-containing, enlarged presynaptic cisternal-like structures in sky mutants after stimulation of SV exocytosis that is not observed in controls (Fig 2 and 3). Expression of a dominant negative version of Rab35 in sky mutants abolishes these structures and restores FM1-43 to a wildtype like distribution, indicating that Skywalker functions to regulate Rab35-mediate trafficking to these structures. Sorting of synaptic membranes as visualised by using an artificial chimeric version of synaptobrevin as ESCRT cargo revealed that levels of this cargo is significantly reduced as compared to wild type animals (Fig 7). Taken together, the results indicate that Rab35 is responsible for directing a subpopulation of synaptic vesicle proteins and membrane for sorting and/or degradation via synaptic endosomes.. Experimental description: Materials and Methods: "Larvae were dissected in HL-3, fixed for 20 min in 3.7% formaldehyde (5 min in Bouin's for anti-VGlut) and labeled with antibodies."
